mnemonics.title: Green IT Technical Measures
mnemonics.content: VSPV-LCSD: Virtualization → Server Consolidation → Power Management → Virtual Desktop → Load Balancing → Containerization → Storage Optimization → Data Deduplication

mnemonics.title: Energy Efficiency Metrics
mnemonics.content: PUE-DC: PUE = Total Facility / IT Equipment (lower is better); DCiE = IT / Total × 100% (higher is better)

mnemonics.title: Virtualization Benefits
mnemonics.content: SPACE: Space saving, Power reduction, Availability improvement, Cost savings, Efficiency enhancement

acronyms.usage: Measures overall data center energy efficiency - lower is better (ideal is 1.0)
Percentage metric showing IT power usage - higher is better (100% is ideal)
CPU power saving technique that adjusts voltage/frequency based on workload
Network standard (802.3az) that reduces power during idle periods
Centralized desktop management reducing client hardware requirements
Energy-efficient storage with no moving parts compared to HDD
Technique to reduce power consumption by managing component states
Critical infrastructure for data center cooling systems

visualTips.visualization: Draw a simple fraction: PUE = Total Facility Power (numerator) / IT Equipment Power (denominator). For PUE 1.5, for every 1.5W delivered to facility, 1W goes to IT - 0.5W is wasted on cooling/lights/etc.
Imagine alternating rows of servers: cold air intake facing one direction (blue arrows), hot exhaust facing opposite (red arrows). Containment creates a tunnel, preventing mixing.
Stack diagram: Physical Server → Hypervisor/Virtualization Layer → Multiple Virtual Machines → Each with its own OS. One physical box becomes many virtual ones.
Side-by-side comparison: Thin Client (small box, 5-15W, all processing on server) vs Desktop PC (tower, 150W+, local processing and storage).
Before: 10 servers at 20% utilization each (200% total capacity used, 1000W consumed). After: 2 servers at 100% utilization (200% capacity used, 200W consumed). Same work, 80% less power.

analogies.analogy: Like a multi-story building - one physical structure (land/building) houses many individual living spaces (apartments), each appearing separate but sharing common infrastructure.
Like fuel efficiency in a car - PUE 2.0 means half your energy goes to the engine (IT), rest to headlights, AC, etc. PUE 1.0 means all energy goes to moving (computing).
Like a television remote - it has minimal electronics but controls a powerful system (TV/Server). All the 'thinking' happens elsewhere.
Like kitchen ventilation - you wouldn't吹 hot stove exhaust back into the cold air intake. Separate the streams for efficiency.
Like carpooling - instead of 5 cars with 1 person each (5 engines burning fuel), use 1 car with 5 people (1 engine, same transportation).
